name: Mangum (ASGI to AWS Lambda Adapter)

auth.notes: Lambda adapter; uses execution role for AWS services

performance.notes: Thin adapter; performance depends on ASGI app

agent_readiness.notes: Adapter to run ASGI apps (FastAPI, Django, Starlette) on AWS Lambda. from mangum import Mangum. handler = Mangum(app). Deploy FastAPI to Lambda behind API Gateway without changes. Supports API Gateway v1/v2 and Lambda Function URLs. lifespan startup/shutdown events. Custom domain with ACM. Serverless deployment of FastAPI apps. Works with Zappa or AWS SAM. The standard way to deploy FastAPI to Lambda.

gotchas: Cold starts can be slow for large ASGI apps
Streaming responses require Lambda Function URLs (not API GW)
Long-running requests limited by Lambda 15-min timeout
best_when: Deploying FastAPI or Django Ninja to AWS Lambda
Serverless REST APIs without managing containers
Event-driven FastAPI handlers behind API Gateway
Python ASGI apps needing Lambda Function URL support
avoid_when: WebSocket APIs (use Lambda + API Gateway WebSocket instead)
summary: Mangum adapts ASGI apps (FastAPI, Django, Starlette) to run on AWS Lambda. handler = Mangum(app) — one line deployment. Supports API Gateway v1/v2 and Lambda Function URLs. The standard way to deploy FastAPI to Lambda without containerization. Works with AWS SAM, Serverless Framework, or manual Lambda config.
